node.title: Uber | Onsite | Split array into k subarrays with min abs diff

topic.title: Uber | Onsite | Split array into k subarrays with min abs diff

post.content:
Given an array `arr` of non-negative integers. You need to split it into `k` contiguous subarrays such that the absolute difference between max sum and min sum is the smallest and output that difference.

**Example:** 
```
Input: arr = [3, 4, 5, 3, 7, 2], k = 3
Output: 2
Explanation: Optimal split is subarray1 = [3, 4], subarray2 = [5, 3], subarray3 = [7, 2]
Min sum = 7 and max sum = 9, hence the answer is |7 - 9| = 2.
```

I think this can be solved using the binary search approach of Painter\'s Partition Problem.\nFirst, find the maximum sum after k partitions and using that we can easily find the min-sum also.\nRefer this to understand the Partition Problem https://jackygao.wordpress.com/2014/09/04/the-painters-partition-problem-part-ii/
DP definitely can solve this problem, but it takes O(NK^2). Not quite sure whether we can use binary search to try another solution.
Doesn\'t this translate to the same optimization constraint as https://leetcode.com/problems/split-array-largest-sum/description/. Does the array have negative integers?

Let X be the maximum sum for any part such that we are able to partition the array into k parts. Let the minimum sum of any part be Y. If X is small, then Y will be high, and if X is high then Y will be small since the total sum is constant, keeping the constraint that Y <= X. Thus if we make X smallest possible then Y will be closest to X, which is what we want. Thus find the X which is minimum among all possibilities. \n\nIt can be found using binary search
what about cutting the array greedily to make the sum as close to [sum of array]/k ?
How are you choosing the subarray size here? please explain.
what were the constraints on N and k? I have uber interview 5 days later\n
